bead.title: integration: prove server-managed workers leave no process leaks in Docker
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The feature is only credible if an automated isolated test proves server-managed worker cleanup leaves no ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants behind. Unit tests are necessary but not sufficient for process-tree cleanup.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214 Stop and :1423 reapWorker are process-cleanup entry points, but existing tests do not run a full server plus managed worker plus fake agent child tree in an isolated environment and assert post-cleanup process state. docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md Testing Strategy requires a Docker-enabled no-leak scenario.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a Docker-backed integration test or scripts/integration/server-managed-workers-docker.sh. Build DDx from current source in the container, create a fixture repo with a deterministic script/fake harness, put fake claude and codex binaries first on PATH, start ddx server, set desired_count=1 through server API or ddx worker CLI, wait for fake children, then exercise explicit stop, watchdog reap, restart, and server shutdown. Assert no managed ddx work, fake claude/codex, or descendant shell/sleep process remains after each cleanup path.

NON-SCOPE
Do not require real Claude/Codex credentials. Do not rely on host tmux or host process state. Do not make the test mandatory on developer machines without Docker; skip clearly there, but provide a CI/release command that runs when Docker is available.
bead.acceptance:
1. scripts/integration/server-managed-workers-docker.sh or TestIntegration_ServerManagedWorker_NoProcessLeaks builds/runs an isolated Docker scenario from current source.
2. The scenario verifies explicit stop leaves no managed ddx work, fake claude, fake codex, shell, or sleep descendants running.
3. The scenario verifies watchdog reap and server shutdown leave no managed worker or fake agent descendants running.
4. The scenario calls stop twice and proves the second call is a no-op with no duplicate bead event and no unrelated process kill.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/integration/... ./internal/server/... -run 'ServerManagedWorker|NoProcessLeaks' passes when Docker is available and skips with a clear message when Docker is unavailable.
6. scripts/integration/server-managed-workers-docker.sh passes on a Docker-enabled host if implemented as a script.
7. make install is run after the code change lands.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
